Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class Order.Builder

com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.Order.Builder

Order

  • Method Details

    • accountId

      public Order.Builder accountId(String accountId)
      The identifier of the account transacting this order

    • accountId

      public Order.Builder accountId(Optional<String> accountId)
      The identifier of the account transacting this order

    • assetId

      public Order.Builder assetId(String assetId)
      Apex Asset ID for this asset. This will not be returned in the initial CreateOrder response and will be available after an order completes validation. If the provided identifier does not match any Apex asset available for trading, an OrderRejectReason of "UNKNOWN_SECURITY" will be returned and the asset_id will not be set.

    • assetId

      public Order.Builder assetId(Optional<String> assetId)
      Apex Asset ID for this asset. This will not be returned in the initial CreateOrder response and will be available after an order completes validation. If the provided identifier does not match any Apex asset available for trading, an OrderRejectReason of "UNKNOWN_SECURITY" will be returned and the asset_id will not be set.

    • assetType

      public Order.Builder assetType(OrderAssetType assetType)
      The type of the asset in this order, which must be one of the following: EQUITY, MUTUAL_FUND, and FIXED_INCOME.

    • assetType

      public Order.Builder assetType(Optional<? extends OrderAssetType> assetType)
      The type of the asset in this order, which must be one of the following: EQUITY, MUTUAL_FUND, and FIXED_INCOME.

    • averagePrices

      public Order.Builder averagePrices(List<TradingExecutedPrice> averagePrices)
      The average prices, as weighted averages, across all executions in this order. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

      When asset_type = EQUITY or MUTUAL_FUND, there will be at most one value present, with a type of PRICE_PER_UNIT. This will have up to 4 decimal places for USD amounts less than $1, and a maximum of two for larger USD amounts.

      When asset_type = FIXED_INCOME, there may be more than one value present which would have a type other than PRICE_PER_UNIT. Price values in PERCENTAGE_OF_PAR will have up to 4 decimal places of precision, and price values measured in yields will support up to 5 decimal places.

    • averagePrices

      public Order.Builder averagePrices(Optional<? extends List<TradingExecutedPrice>> averagePrices)
      The average prices, as weighted averages, across all executions in this order. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

      When asset_type = EQUITY or MUTUAL_FUND, there will be at most one value present, with a type of PRICE_PER_UNIT. This will have up to 4 decimal places for USD amounts less than $1, and a maximum of two for larger USD amounts.

      When asset_type = FIXED_INCOME, there may be more than one value present which would have a type other than PRICE_PER_UNIT. Price values in PERCENTAGE_OF_PAR will have up to 4 decimal places of precision, and price values measured in yields will support up to 5 decimal places.

    • brokerCapacity

      public Order.Builder brokerCapacity(OrderBrokerCapacity brokerCapacity)
      Defaults to "AGENCY" if not specified. For Equities: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Mutual Funds: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Fixed Income: Either "AGENCY" or "PRINCIPAL" are allowed.

    • brokerCapacity

      public Order.Builder brokerCapacity(Optional<? extends OrderBrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity)
      Defaults to "AGENCY" if not specified. For Equities: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Mutual Funds: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Fixed Income: Either "AGENCY" or "PRINCIPAL" are allowed.

    • cancelReason

      public Order.Builder cancelReason(String cancelReason)
      Used to explain why an order is canceled

    • cancelReason

      public Order.Builder cancelReason(Optional<String> cancelReason)
      Used to explain why an order is canceled

    • cancelRejectedReason

      public Order.Builder cancelRejectedReason(CancelRejectedReason cancelRejectedReason)
      Used to denote when a cancel request has been rejected.

    • cancelRejectedReason

      public Order.Builder cancelRejectedReason(Optional<? extends CancelRejectedReason> cancelRejectedReason)
      Used to denote when a cancel request has been rejected.

    • clientOrderId

      public Order.Builder clientOrderId(String clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • clientOrderId

      public Order.Builder clientOrderId(Optional<String> clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • clientReceivedTime

      public Order.Builder clientReceivedTime(OffsetDateTime clientReceivedTime)
      Required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. A value may be provided for non-Equity orders, and will be remembered, but valid timestamps will have no impact on how they are processed.

    • clientReceivedTime

      public Order.Builder clientReceivedTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime)
      Required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. A value may be provided for non-Equity orders, and will be remembered, but valid timestamps will have no impact on how they are processed.

    • commission

      public Order.Builder commission(OrderCommission commission)
      A custom commission to be applied to this order. When specifying an AMOUNT type, the value represents a notional amount measured in the currency of the order.

    • commission

      public Order.Builder commission(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OrderCommission> commission)
      A custom commission to be applied to this order. When specifying an AMOUNT type, the value represents a notional amount measured in the currency of the order.

    • createTime

      public Order.Builder createTime(OffsetDateTime createTime)
      Time of the order creation

    • createTime

      public Order.Builder createTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime)
      Time of the order creation

    • cumulativeNotionalValue

      public Order.Builder cumulativeNotionalValue(CumulativeNotionalValue cumulativeNotionalValue)
      The sum of order quantity & price, across all fills, reported in the currency specified in the order. (This will be rounded to 2 decimal places for USD currencies). Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • cumulativeNotionalValue

      public Order.Builder cumulativeNotionalValue(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends CumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue)
      The sum of order quantity & price, across all fills, reported in the currency specified in the order. (This will be rounded to 2 decimal places for USD currencies). Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • currencyCode

      public Order.Builder currencyCode(String currencyCode)
      Defaults to "USD". Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • currencyCode

      public Order.Builder currencyCode(Optional<String> currencyCode)
      Defaults to "USD". Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • executions

      public Order.Builder executions(List<TradingExecutions> executions)
      The execution-level details that compose this order

    • executions

      public Order.Builder executions(Optional<? extends List<TradingExecutions>> executions)
      The execution-level details that compose this order

    • fees

      public Order.Builder fees(List<TradingFee> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this order. Only the BROKER_FEE type is supported.

    • fees

      public Order.Builder fees(Optional<? extends List<TradingFee>> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this order. Only the BROKER_FEE type is supported.

    • filledQuantity

      public Order.Builder filledQuantity(FilledQuantity filledQuantity)
      The summed quantity value across all fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • filledQuantity

      public Order.Builder filledQuantity(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends FilledQuantity> filledQuantity)
      The summed quantity value across all fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • identifier

      public Order.Builder identifier(String identifier)
      Identifier of the asset (of the type specified in `identifier_type`).

    • identifier

      public Order.Builder identifier(Optional<String> identifier)
      Identifier of the asset (of the type specified in `identifier_type`).

    • identifierIssuingRegionCode

      public Order.Builder identifierIssuingRegionCode(String identifierIssuingRegionCode)
      A string attribute denoting the country of issuance or where the asset is trading. Only available for Mutual Fund orders. Defaults to US, when trading non US mutual funds this field must be provided Complies with ISO-3166 Alpha-2 Codes

    • identifierIssuingRegionCode

      public Order.Builder identifierIssuingRegionCode(Optional<String> identifierIssuingRegionCode)
      A string attribute denoting the country of issuance or where the asset is trading. Only available for Mutual Fund orders. Defaults to US, when trading non US mutual funds this field must be provided Complies with ISO-3166 Alpha-2 Codes

    • identifierType

      public Order.Builder identifierType(OrderIdentifierType identifierType)
      The identifier type of the asset being ordered. For Equities: only SYMBOL is supported For Mutual Funds: only SYMBOL and CUSIP are supported For Fixed Income: only CUSIP and ISIN are supported

    • identifierType

      public Order.Builder identifierType(Optional<? extends OrderIdentifierType> identifierType)
      The identifier type of the asset being ordered. For Equities: only SYMBOL is supported For Mutual Funds: only SYMBOL and CUSIP are supported For Fixed Income: only CUSIP and ISIN are supported

    • lastUpdateTime

      public Order.Builder lastUpdateTime(OffsetDateTime lastUpdateTime)
      Time of the last order update

    • lastUpdateTime

      public Order.Builder lastUpdateTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> lastUpdateTime)
      Time of the last order update

    • letterOfIntent

      public Order.Builder letterOfIntent(LetterOfIntent letterOfIntent)
      Letter of Intent (LOI). An LOI allows investors to receive sales charge discounts based on a commitment to buy a specified monetary amount of shares over a period of time, usually 13 months.

    • letterOfIntent

      public Order.Builder letterOfIntent(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends LetterOfIntent> letterOfIntent)
      Letter of Intent (LOI). An LOI allows investors to receive sales charge discounts based on a commitment to buy a specified monetary amount of shares over a period of time, usually 13 months.

    • limitPrice

      public Order.Builder limitPrice(LimitPrice limitPrice)
      The limit price for this order.

    • limitPrice

      public Order.Builder limitPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends LimitPrice> limitPrice)
      The limit price for this order.

    • maxSellQuantity

      public Order.Builder maxSellQuantity(MaxSellQuantity maxSellQuantity)
      The maximum number of shares to be sold if this is a notional SELL order of an Equity asset type. (Prohibited for other side or asset_type inputs.)

      This will only be recognized for clients configured not to use OMS checks. When specified, must be greater than 0 and can't exceed 5 decimal places.

    • maxSellQuantity

      public Order.Builder maxSellQuantity(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends MaxSellQuantity> maxSellQuantity)
      The maximum number of shares to be sold if this is a notional SELL order of an Equity asset type. (Prohibited for other side or asset_type inputs.)

      This will only be recognized for clients configured not to use OMS checks. When specified, must be greater than 0 and can't exceed 5 decimal places.

    • name

      public Order.Builder name(String name)
      System generated name of the order.

    • name

      public Order.Builder name(Optional<String> name)
      System generated name of the order.

    • notionalValue

      public Order.Builder notionalValue(NotionalValue notionalValue)
      Notional quantity of the order, measured in USD. Maximum 2 decimal place precision. For Equities: This represents the maximum amount to be spent. The final order may may have a smaller notional amount. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for BUY orders. The order will be transacted at the full notional amount specified. For Fixed Income: Not supported, you must specify a `quantity` value.

    • notionalValue

      public Order.Builder notionalValue(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends NotionalValue> notionalValue)
      Notional quantity of the order, measured in USD. Maximum 2 decimal place precision. For Equities: This represents the maximum amount to be spent. The final order may may have a smaller notional amount. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for BUY orders. The order will be transacted at the full notional amount specified. For Fixed Income: Not supported, you must specify a `quantity` value.

    • orderDate

      public Order.Builder orderDate(OrderDate orderDate)
      The date on which the order will go to the market: must either be "today" or the next valid trading day. If the current day is not a valid trading day, then the next valid market day must be specified. If the current time is within 5 minutes prior to market close, the next valid market day may be specified. If the current time is after market close, and before midnight Eastern, then the next valid market day must be specified. In all other cases, the current day, Eastern must be specified.

    • orderDate

      public Order.Builder orderDate(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OrderDate> orderDate)
      The date on which the order will go to the market: must either be "today" or the next valid trading day. If the current day is not a valid trading day, then the next valid market day must be specified. If the current time is within 5 minutes prior to market close, the next valid market day may be specified. If the current time is after market close, and before midnight Eastern, then the next valid market day must be specified. In all other cases, the current day, Eastern must be specified.

    • orderId

      public Order.Builder orderId(String orderId)
      System generated unique id for the order.

    • orderId

      public Order.Builder orderId(Optional<String> orderId)
      System generated unique id for the order.

    • orderRejectedReason

      public Order.Builder orderRejectedReason(OrderRejectedReason orderRejectedReason)
      When an order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    • orderRejectedReason

      public Order.Builder orderRejectedReason(Optional<? extends OrderRejectedReason> orderRejectedReason)
      When an order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    • orderStatus

      public Order.Builder orderStatus(OrderStatus orderStatus)
      The processing status of the order

    • orderStatus

      public Order.Builder orderStatus(Optional<? extends OrderStatus> orderStatus)
      The processing status of the order

    • orderType

      public Order.Builder orderType(OrderOrderType orderType)
      The execution type of this order. For Equities: MARKET, LIMIT, or STOP are supported. For Mutual Funds: only MARKET is supported. For Fixed Income: only LIMIT is supported.

    • orderType

      public Order.Builder orderType(Optional<? extends OrderOrderType> orderType)
      The execution type of this order. For Equities: MARKET, LIMIT, or STOP are supported. For Mutual Funds: only MARKET is supported. For Fixed Income: only LIMIT is supported.

    • prevailingMarketPrice

      public Order.Builder prevailingMarketPrice(OrderPrevailingMarketPrice prevailingMarketPrice)
      The prevailing market price, calculated as a weighted average of the fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

    • prevailingMarketPrice

      public Order.Builder prevailingMarketPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OrderPrevailingMarketPrice> prevailingMarketPrice)
      The prevailing market price, calculated as a weighted average of the fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

    • quantity

      public Order.Builder quantity(OrderQuantity quantity)
      Numeric quantity of the order. For Equities: Represents the number of shares, must be greater than zero and may not exceed 5 decimal places. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for SELL orders. Represents the number of shares, up to a maximum of 3 decimal places. For Fixed Income: Represents the par (face-value) amount being ordered, and may not exceed two decimal places for USD-based currencies. Either a quantity or notional_value MUST be specified (but not both).

    • quantity

      public Order.Builder quantity(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OrderQuantity> quantity)
      Numeric quantity of the order. For Equities: Represents the number of shares, must be greater than zero and may not exceed 5 decimal places. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for SELL orders. Represents the number of shares, up to a maximum of 3 decimal places. For Fixed Income: Represents the par (face-value) amount being ordered, and may not exceed two decimal places for USD-based currencies. Either a quantity or notional_value MUST be specified (but not both).

    • rightsOfAccumulation

      public Order.Builder rightsOfAccumulation(RightsOfAccumulation rightsOfAccumulation)
      Rights of Accumulation (ROA). An ROA allows an investor to aggregate their own fund shares with the holdings of certain related parties toward achieving the investment thresholds at which sales charge discounts become available.

    • rightsOfAccumulation

      public Order.Builder rightsOfAccumulation(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends RightsOfAccumulation> rightsOfAccumulation)
      Rights of Accumulation (ROA). An ROA allows an investor to aggregate their own fund shares with the holdings of certain related parties toward achieving the investment thresholds at which sales charge discounts become available.

    • side

      public Order.Builder side(OrderSide side)
      The side of this order.

    • side

      public Order.Builder side(Optional<? extends OrderSide> side)
      The side of this order.

    • specialReportingInstructions

      public Order.Builder specialReportingInstructions(List<OrderSpecialReportingInstructions> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • specialReportingInstructions

      public Order.Builder specialReportingInstructions(Optional<? extends List<OrderSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • stopPrice

      public Order.Builder stopPrice(StopPrice stopPrice)
      The stop price for this order. Only allowed for equities, when the side is SELL.

    • stopPrice

      public Order.Builder stopPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends StopPrice> stopPrice)
      The stop price for this order. Only allowed for equities, when the side is SELL.

    • timeInForce

      public Order.Builder timeInForce(OrderTimeInForce timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate that the system capture the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • timeInForce

      public Order.Builder timeInForce(Optional<? extends OrderTimeInForce> timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate that the system capture the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • tradingStrategy

      public Order.Builder tradingStrategy(OrderTradingStrategy tradingStrategy)
      Which TradingStrategy Session to trade in, defaults to 'CORE'. Only available for Equity orders.

    • tradingStrategy

      public Order.Builder tradingStrategy(Optional<? extends OrderTradingStrategy> tradingStrategy)
      Which TradingStrategy Session to trade in, defaults to 'CORE'. Only available for Equity orders.

    • build

      public Order build()
